questions.explanation: `val` creates an immutable reference (read-only). `var` is mutable, `const` is for compile-time constants, and `lateinit` is for deferred initialization of `var` properties.
questions.question: In Kotlin, which keyword declares a read-only variable (cannot be reassigned)?

questions.explanation: Android Studio is the official IDE for Android development, providing code editing, build tools integration, emulator support, debugging, and profiling.

options.A: Designing and building Android apps in an IDE
options.B: Running Android apps only on physical devices (not emulators)
options.D: Hosting REST APIs for mobile apps

questions.question: What is Android Studio primarily used for?

questions.explanation: The Android platform for a given API level includes `android.jar`, which contains the Android framework APIs used at compile time.

options.A: Platform (android.jar) for a specific API level

questions.question: Which Android SDK component provides the core Android APIs and libraries used to compile apps?

questions.explanation: Git is a distributed version control system used for tracking changes, branching, merging, and collaboration.
questions.question: In software configuration management, which tool is most commonly used to track code changes and collaborate via branches and merges?

questions.explanation: `LazyColumn` only composes and lays out visible items (plus a small buffer), making it suitable for large lists.
questions.question: In Jetpack Compose, which composable is specifically designed to efficiently display a long scrolling list of items?

questions.explanation: The ViewModel is designed to hold UI-related data and logic in a lifecycle-aware way and survive configuration changes.
questions.question: In MVVM, where should UI state and business logic that survives configuration changes (like rotation) typically live?

questions.explanation: Retrofit uses an annotated interface to define endpoints; with OkHttp and a converter (Moshi/Gson), it can map JSON to Kotlin data classes. Network calls should not run on the main thread.

options.A: Define a Retrofit interface with suspend functions and use a JSON converter (e.g., Moshi/Gson)
options.B: Use Room DAO methods to fetch HTTP responses
options.C: Use LiveData to open a raw TCP socket directly
options.D: Write all network calls on the main thread for simplicity

questions.question: You need to call a REST API using Retrofit with OkHttp and parse JSON into Kotlin objects. Which approach is most appropriate?

questions.explanation: `repeatOnLifecycle` ties collection to the lifecycle state and automatically cancels/restarts collection, preventing leaks and wasted work when the UI is stopped.

options.C: Collect the Flow in Application.onCreate() and store the latest value in a static field
options.D: Run flow.collect() on the main thread without any lifecycle awareness

questions.question: You collect a Flow in a Fragment to update the UI. To avoid leaks and stop collecting when the view is not STARTED, which pattern is best?

questions.explanation: A repository coordinating Room (single source of truth) and network refresh is a common offline-first pattern. UI observes Room via Flow/LiveData; network updates are persisted to Room.

options.A: Repository exposes a Flow from Room; a refresh function fetches via Retrofit and writes to Room; ViewModel collects the Room Flow for UI state
options.B: ViewModel calls Retrofit on the main thread and directly updates the UI without caching
options.C: Activity reads SQLite directly and also performs network calls, bypassing a repository
options.D: Store all server responses only in SharedPreferences and never use Room

questions.question: You want offline-first behavior: show cached data immediately from Room, then refresh from the network and update the cache. Which design best fits MVVM + Jetpack components?

questions.explanation: Installing the module in `SingletonComponent` and scoping with `@Singleton` ensures one shared Retrofit instance across the application.

options.A: Create a @Module with @InstallIn(SingletonComponent::class) and a @Provides or @Binds method annotated with @Singleton
options.B: Instantiate Retrofit inside every Activity to ensure fresh instances
options.C: Put Retrofit in a companion object without DI to avoid configuration
options.D: Provide Retrofit in a @Module installed in ActivityComponent without any scope

questions.question: In a multi-module Android app using Hilt, you need to provide a single instance of Retrofit shared across the app. Where and how should it be provided?
